## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is a pubcast shot. Wooo baby! I'm John Loomer. Let's go! So we've heard a lot about how Facebook website custom audiences are going to be smaller and maybe less effective in some cases due to iOS 14 and opting out of tracking and being removed from those audiences. But the thing is there's so many ways that Facebook can replace that power because the audiences that aren't impacted are related to engagement and activity within the app and on Facebook itself. So what if Facebook expanded the engagement custom audiences? So you can create audiences out of anyone who shared your posts, who commented on your posts, or who reacted to your posts, or even a specific reaction like the love reaction if you know that that's most powerful. Strangely, this was all available in Facebook analytics previously, but that's no longer a thing. What if you could create audiences based on that type of engagement with specific posts or ads and then target or exclude accordingly? What if there are more granular instant experience audiences beyond just opening an instant experience or clicking a link within it but performing another specific type of engagement within it? What if you could create an audience out of people who were shown a post or ad and that way you could then show ads to people who haven't previously seen it yet? And or you could control frequency by excluding those who already saw a poster ad. What if you could create an audience based on frequency of engagement so people who message you frequently, people who click your links frequently, people who comment on your posts frequently? What if you could also create audiences out of frequency of impressions so then you could exclude only those who saw a post a certain number of times during the past X days? And I'm talking about optimizing foreign action like conversions where you could then control that frequency, not just with reach optimization. What if now that we got Facebook podcasts, you create audiences out of people who have listened to prior episodes, people who have subscribed to your show and then show ads accordingly? This would be pretty awesome Facebook. You can do it. This has been a pubcast shot. Guess what? Your questions can be answered as a part of a pubcast shot. Just submit it to me at infoatjohnlummer.com. Make sure you put in the subject line, pubcast shot question, and maybe, just maybe, I'll answer your question on a pubcast shot.
